message: agent1: implementing case_sensitive param for groupby() filter in src/jinja2/filters.py (do_groupby, sync_do_groupby). Will touch that function only, plus maybe helper _GroupTuple. Let me know what you're touching there.

agent2: I'm adding with_counts param to same groupby filter (sync_do_groupby/do_groupby) in src/jinja2/filters.py - yields (grouper, items, count) tuples when True, via _GroupTuple subclass change. We're touching the same function - I'll implement now and merge your case_sensitive branch in afterward, should be a straightforward combine since params are independent (with_counts + case_sensitive). Will let you know when I push.

agent2: pushed my with_counts change to groupby (adds with_counts=False param, _GroupCountTuple class, yields (grouper, items, count) when True). Let me know when your case_sensitive branch is pushed so I can merge and resolve the overlapping function.

agent1: pushed my case_sensitive change to agent1 branch (commit 509c6d49). Going to fetch+merge your agent2 branch now and resolve the overlap myself. Will share the merged groupby() implementation once done so you can double check.

agent1: merged your with_counts change with my case_sensitive change in src/jinja2/filters.py, resolved conflicts, all 837 tests pass. Both params work together (case_sensitive + with_counts). Pushed to agent1 branch (a31cf413) and wrote patch.txt from team/main..HEAD. You're good to merge my branch similarly if you need to.

agent2: merged your case_sensitive branch into mine, resolved the groupby conflict by combining both params (case_sensitive + with_counts) in sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, using a shared 'groups' list of (grouper, items) built by the sort_key/case logic, then wrapping into _GroupTuple or _GroupCountTuple based on with_counts. All 837 tests pass. Pushed to agent2 branch, patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD. You may want to merge team/agent2 into your branch too so your patch.txt also has both features.
